title: Volume with fractions
description: Practice finding the volume of rectangular prisms that have fractional side lengths.

assessment_items.question_content:
**What is the volume of this rectangular prism?**



[[☃ image 1]]

[[☃ numeric-input 1]] $\text{cm}^3$
hints: The volume of a box is the product of its width, length, and height.

Therefore, 

$\begin{align}\text{volume} &= \dfrac{4}{3}\cdot\dfrac{3}{5}\cdot 3  \  \text{cm}^3 
\\\\
&=\dfrac{12}{5} \  \text{cm}^3 \end{align}$

options.alt: A rectangular prism.  The length is 3 centimeters. The width is 4 thirds centimeters. The height is 3 fifths centimeter.

assessment_items.question_content:
**What is the volume of this rectangular prism?**



[[☃ image 1]]

[[☃ numeric-input 1]] $\text{cm}^3$
hints: The volume of a box is the product of its width, length, and height.

Therefore, 

$\begin{align}\text{volume} &= \dfrac{2}{5}\cdot\dfrac{2}{5}\cdot \dfrac{2}{5}  \  \text{cm}^3 \\\\
&=\dfrac{8}{125} \  \text{cm}^3 \end{align}$

options.alt: A rectangular prism.  The length is 2 fifths centimeter. The width is 2 fifths centimeter. The height is 2 fifths centimeter.

assessment_items.question_content:
**What is the volume of this rectangular prism?**




[[☃ image 1]]

[[☃ numeric-input 1]] $\text{cm}^3$
hints: The volume of a box is the product of its width, length, and height.

Therefore, 

$\begin{align}\text{volume} &= \dfrac{3}{2}\cdot\dfrac{5}{3}\cdot \dfrac{1}{4}  \  \text{cm}^3 \\\\
&=\dfrac{5}{8} \  \text{cm}^3 \end{align}$

options.alt: A rectangular prism.  The length is 3 halves centimeters. The width is 1 fourth centimeter. The height is 5 thirds centimeters.

assessment_items.question_content:
**What is the volume of this rectangular prism?**

Therefore,

options.alt: A rectangular prism.  The length is 1 half centimeter. The width is 4 centimeters. The height is 5 halves centimeters.
